text: Law Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador
The Law Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador is heritage partner for the History of Law
and the Courts, www.heritage.nf.ca on the Newfoundland and Labrador Heritage Web Site at
Memorial University. This public history project presents the province's rich heritage in a format
that combines text, graphics, audio and video. Six major areas, involving heritage partners, are
featured: Natural Environment, Aboriginal Peoples, Exploration and Settlement, Government and
Politics, Society, Economy and Culture, and the Arts. In addition to this cultural and academic
knowledge-base being available on the Internet to university students and the general public, as a
further educational resource high school students are able to access high school curriculum
specific modules on the law and constitutional issues.
The Law Foundation, established in February, 1980, provides grants to advance
public understanding of the law and access to legal services.
The Law Foundation, Law Society and Government of Newfoundland and Labrador
extended gratitude to Hon. P. Derek Lewis, Q.C., former Chair, for his years of service as Chair.
Mr. M. Francis O'Dea, Q.C., Chair and Mrs. Barbara O'Dea, current governors and invited guests,
in particular former governors from the first appointed Board, and their spouses/guests
joined Hon. P. Derek Lewis, Q.C., and Mrs. Grace Lewis at a Dinner in honour of his service
and retirement as Chair, from the first meeting of the Board on February 26, 1979,
when the work began to allow for the proclamation of Part IV of the Law Society Act
on February 1, 1980, establishing the Law Foundation,
to August, 2005, following the Law Foundation's 25th Anniversary.
Current First Governors in 1979 of the Law Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador:
M. Francis O'Dea, Q.C., first Secretary, 1979;
Chair, 2005 - present Norman J. Whalen, Q.C., 1979;

The funds were provided from the legal education, research and law reform component
of the Reserve Account. (The Canadian Legal Conference is a comprehensive
continuing legal education program with seminars and debates on significant legal issues.
The Law Foundation sponsored this event for the same amount when it was first held
in this province in St. John's in August, 1998.)
